name: Jabalon and Ermita de San Cristobal - running from Jabaloyas
name_original: Jabalón y Ermita de San Cristóbal – running desde Jabaloyas
region_intro: Jogging around Jabaloyas offers a unique experience in the Sierra de Albarracín, Teruel, Spain. The region is characterized by its mountainous terrain, dense pine forests, and varied elevations, providing a natural setting for active exploration. Joggers can navigate routes that traverse deep canyons, follow river valleys, and pass by natural monuments such as lagoons and waterfalls. This environment, situated at over 1,400 meters, combines challenging ascents with serene natural beauty. Best jogging routes around Jabaloyas The most popular jogging route is Ojos del Cabriel and cascada del Molino de San Pedro – circular by La Herrería Waterfall, a 4.6 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes 55 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural springs of the Cabriel River and a picturesque waterfall. Another top favourite among local runners is Mirador del Alto de las Peñas – circular from Terriente, a moderate 6.7 miles (10.7 km) path. This route offers panoramic views from the Alto de las Peñas, winding through the varied landscape near Terriente. Local runners also love the Dornaque to Peña del Hierro Viewpoint Trail, a 2.9 miles (4.6 km) trail leading through forested areas to a scenic viewpoint, often completed in about 33 minutes. Jogging around Jabaloyas is defined by mountainous terrain, dense pine forests, and dramatic canyons. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 10 runners have used komoot to explore Jabaloyas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Jabaloyas?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;6 dedicated running routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Jabaloyas listed in this guide, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes explore the region's unique mountainous terrain, dense pine forests, and dramatic canyons.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging trails in Jabaloyas?
A: Yes, Jabaloyas offers routes suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983280110/dornaque-to-pena-del-hierro-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dornaque to Peña del Hierro Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.9-mile (4.6 km) path, perfect for a gentle run through forested areas to a scenic viewpoint.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Jabaloyas area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the routes around Jabaloyas are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983156660/ojos-del-cabriel-and-cascada-del-molino-de-san-pedro-circular-by-la-herreria-waterfall" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel and cascada del Molino de San Pedro – circular by La Herrería Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 4.6-mile (7.5 km) loop exploring natural springs and a picturesque waterfall.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see while jogging in Jabaloyas?
A: Jabaloyas is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter stunning features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrería Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/604533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro Mill Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the natural springs of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4547251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features serene wetlands such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/756481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Bezas&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4483018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa del Pinar&lt;/a&gt;, all set amidst dense pine forests and varied mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31895529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calicanto Waterfall – Gorges of the Ebrón River loop from Tormón&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 9.5-mile (15.3 km) route with significant elevation changes, offering a rewarding experience through dramatic gorges.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for running routes in Jabaloyas?
A: Running routes in Jabaloyas vary in length and duration. You'll find paths ranging from approximately 2.9 miles (4.6 km) taking about 30-40 minutes, up to 9.5 miles (15.3 km) which can take over 2 hours, depending on your pace and the terrain. The average route is around 4-7 miles (6-11 km).

Q: What makes Jabaloyas a unique destination for outdoor running?
A: Jabaloyas stands out due to its pristine natural environment, situated at over 1,400 meters altitude in the Sierra de Albarracín. Runners can enjoy diverse landscapes, from dense Scots and Corsican pine forests to deep canyons carved by tributaries of the Ebrón River. The area's Starlight Stellar Park certification also highlights its untouched nature and clear skies, contributing to a serene running experience.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, for breathtaking vistas, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983323510/mirador-del-alto-de-las-penas-circular-from-terriente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Alto de las Peñas – circular from Terriente&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 6.7-mile (10.7 km) path offers expansive panoramic views from the Alto de las Peñas, winding through the varied landscape near Terriente.

Q: What is the terrain like on the running trails around Jabaloyas?
A: The terrain around Jabaloyas is characterized by its mountainous nature, featuring varied elevations with both ascents and descents. You'll primarily run through dense pine forests, but also encounter river valleys and dramatic canyons. The presence of abundant springs ensures humid soil, supporting rich vegetation and adding to the refreshing atmosphere.

Q: Can I combine my run with exploring local landmarks or historical sites?
A: While the focus is on natural beauty, the extensive trail network in the Sierra de Albarracín often connects ancient Iberian settlements and viewpoints. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983268958/jabalon-and-ermita-de-san-cristobal-running-from-jabaloyas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jabalon and Ermita de San Cristobal - running from Jabaloyas&lt;/a&gt; route takes you past the Ermita de San Cristobal, offering a glimpse into the local heritage amidst your run.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The diverse ecosystem of Jabaloyas is home to a variety of wildlife. You might spot raptors and other birds, and during autumn, the area is known for the 'berrea' (deer rutting season) in the ancient pine forests, offering a unique natural spectacle.
tips: The top of Jabalón at 1692 meters above sea level. offers wonderful views in addition to its interesting witch legends. The Hermitage of San Cristóbal is also located there, which adds a special touch to the landscape.
